mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by retail sales (RSAFS), signals robust consumer demand which drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 12-month rolling correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is less than 0.1, or if RSAFS declines while SPX rises more than 50% of the time over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above the 4.5% threshold signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending power and corporate profit expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in more than 60% of triggered instances (UNRATE > 4.5%) the SPX delivers positive 5-day returns, or if the average negative return is not statistically significantly different from zero at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus year-over-year CPI inflation) is low or negative, monetary policy is accommodative, compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations. The combination of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL jointly determines the extent of accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the composite spread (real rate) shows a negative rank IC with future 20-day returns over any 3-year window, or if the interaction term in a linear model is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, declining housing starts (HOUST) indicate that higher interest rates are cooling the housing market, which leads to lower economic growth and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If within tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4%) the correlation between HOUST changes and forward SPX returns is positive or not significantly negative, or if the regime-conditioned signal fails to outperform a simple 50/50 coin flip over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal imminent economic contraction, as employers reduce headcount, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If more than 50% of instances with PAYEMS decline are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the average return difference between decline and non-decline months is less than 1% in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of industrial production (INDPRO) to retail sales (RSAFS) captures the balance between supply and demand. A rising ratio (supply outpacing demand) suggests inventory accumulation and future production cuts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rolling 12-month correlation between the INDPRO/RSAFS ratio and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is greater than 0.1, or if the signal does not produce a positive Sharpe ratio over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: If the correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is less than 0.2 over any 10-year rolling window, or if the signal produces negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3%, markets anticipate tighter monetary policy, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If more than 50% of inflation-triggered events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the average excess return over non-triggered periods is not negative and statistically significant at the 90% confidence level.
